title: Is Evangelion 64 fun
wikitext:
[]: Is Evangelion 64 fun?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sat Dec 28, 2013 9:44 am How enjoyable is the Evangelion Nintendo 64 game in terms of control and gameplay? Is it at least something that will pass the time on a rainy day, or is it only worth getting if you want to build up your Evangelion or Nintendo collection?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Dec 28, 2013 10:56 am I have it. It's probably the only Evangelion game that's actually playable. That's not saying very much. I feel fairly sure when I say that they've yet to make an Evangelion game that is genuinely fun. You're probably better off getting a copy of Zone of the Enders instead. This review may be helpful: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IQxYiJ8vUxI

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaLangley » Sat Dec 28, 2013 11:02 am Its fun ! I Like Battle Orchestra for PSP/PS2 too.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Sun Dec 29, 2013 1:11 am Short answer: No Long answer: No.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Jan 11, 2014 9:36 am Pretty much every stage/angel battle is played on a different way. Personally i found it pretty ok in terms of gameplay and thought it was acceptably fun and interesting. Not a great or outstanding game by any means but i think it would be worth your while.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Sun Jan 12, 2014 8:24 pm It's more fun than Battle Orchestra. The battles with Sachiel, Evas MP and Kaworu are really fun stages Ramiel stage might be a bit frustrating...

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Jan 15, 2014 3:24 am Because I am terrible at games in general, I found it to be frustrating very quickly, and subsequently dull. I, personally, would not recommend it.

[]:             Quote Post by Dodgy » Wed May 28, 2014 8:27 am I played it a few years ago and found it to recreate many of the battle scenes well. If I came across it for a good price I'd definitely snap it up, just means a NTSC Nintendo 64 would be needed to play it.
